steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Private Media Authorization · Theory Private Media Authoriza…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 4119 during a regional recovery and budget exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate CloudFront ranges, progressive layout, and accessible media evidence; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, align manifest and segment authorization, signed cookies or URLs, CORS, key delivery, expiry, seeking, and leakage boundaries.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Private Media Authorization contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Build Private Media Authorization evidence · Theory Private Media Au…
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-private-media-authorization-gdbi0d" that uses case 9147 to validate CloudFront ranges, progressive layout, and accessible media evidence.
